name: ICP Definition Clarity
probe: How clearly defined is the Ideal Customer Profile?

levels.description: No defined ICP. Selling to anyone with a pulse.
Multi-tier ICP (Tier 1/2/3) with clear fit criteria.
ICP validated against closed-won data, refreshed quarterly, with anti-fit exclusions.

name: Positioning & One-Liner
probe: How sharp is the value proposition and differentiation?

levels.description: Differentiated position, supported by proof points.
Owned narrative (POV-led / category-shaping), strong competitive moat.

name: Website — Conversion Readiness
probe: Does the website convert traffic into pipeline?

levels.description: Brochure site, no CTAs or lead capture.
Segmented landing pages, lead capture, live chat.
Signal-driven site (visitor de-anon + routed CTAs + intent personalisation).

name: Social Proof Library
probe: How robust is the social proof arsenal?

levels.description: 10+ case studies mapped to ICP segments with metrics.
Continuous case-study engine + peer-review proof (G2, awards, analyst mentions).

name: Founder / Exec LinkedIn Presence
probe: Are senior leaders publishing consistently on LinkedIn?

levels.description: Founder-led content as primary TOFU. 25K+ followers. Meetings booked directly from content.

name: Company Content Cadence
probe: What does the content engine look like beyond social?

levels.description: Multi-format program (blog + podcast + video + newsletter) on schedule.
Content atomised from sales conversations, distributed across all channels, tied to pipeline.

name: Visual Brand System
probe: Is the brand visually coherent across all surfaces?

levels.description: Basic logo and color, ad-hoc design.
Design system with templates for social, sales, web.
Mature brand identity, recognised in category, design ops in place.

name: Category Narrative
probe: Is the company defining, defending, or following a category?

levels.description: Actively framing a sub-category with owned vocabulary.
Category creator — analysts and market reference them.

name: CRM as Source of Truth
probe: Is the CRM the definitive record for accounts and contacts?

levels.description: No CRM, or CRM not used.
CRM exists but duplicates, missing fields, ignored.
CRM used by sales but not synced to marketing/CS.
Central CRM with enforced hygiene, dedupe rules, mandatory fields.
Dual-CRM architecture (mkt + sales) or single source with full bi-directional sync, weekly audit.

name: TAM Mapping
probe: Is the full addressable market mapped and in the CRM?

levels.description: Rough count ('there are 10K companies like this').
TAM list built, but static and stale.
TAM mapped, segmented by tier, refreshed quarterly.
Full scored TAM in CRM (fit + tier), living list, continuous enrichment.

name: Account Firmographic Enrichment
probe: How deep is the firmographic data layer on each account?

levels.description: Enriched from a single source (Apollo or ZoomInfo).
Custom data points (funding, tech stack, growth signals) auto-refreshed.

name: Contact Enrichment — Email + Phone Waterfall
probe: How reliable is contact data for outbound?

levels.description: Verified emails (LeadMagic / BounceBan) on outbound list.
Waterfall enrichment (3+ providers) with validation step.
Full waterfall (email + mobile) with re-verification on every outbound batch.

name: ICP Fit Scoring
probe: Is every account/contact scored for ICP fit?

levels.description: No scoring — all accounts treated equal.
Multi-factor score (firmographics + tech + intent) recalculated regularly.
ML-based scoring calibrated against closed-won, surfaced in CRM.

name: Cross-Tool Data Sync
probe: Does data flow cleanly across the GTM stack?

levels.description: Tools don't talk to each other.
Bi-directional sync across CRM, sequencer, ads, enrichment.
Full orchestration layer (n8n/Make/Zapier) with monitoring and error handling.

name: Data Coverage vs TAM
probe: What % of the TAM is fully enriched and actionable?

levels.description: 90%+ with decision-maker coverage and ongoing refresh.

name: Data Decay Management
probe: How is data kept fresh against 22%/yr natural decay?

levels.description: Real-time signals — job/role/company changes trigger CRM updates.

name: Buying Intent Signals (3rd Party)
probe: Are 3rd-party intent signals captured and wired into the CRM?

levels.description: Single intent source (Bombora or 6sense) in CRM.
Multi-source intent (G2 + Bombora + 6sense) with topic tracking.
Intent composited with fit score, triggering account-level actions.

name: Website Visitor De-anonymization
probe: Are anonymous visitors being identified company-by-company?

levels.description: De-anon solution (RB2B / Warmly / Vector) identifying companies.
De-anon + contact enrichment + auto-routing to outbound, ads, and CSM.

name: LinkedIn Engagement Capture
probe: Are LinkedIn post engagers and profile visitors captured?

levels.description: Post engagers scraped (LeadMagic) but not actioned.
Post + profile visitor capture, enriched, routed by ICP fit.
Every engagement triggers a personalised play. Full engagement pipeline.

name: Trigger Signals — Hiring / Funding / Tech
probe: Are leading-indicator signals monitored?

levels.description: All signals composited, scored by urgency, triggering timely outbound.

name: Account Scoring — Fit × Intent
probe: Are accounts scored by both fit AND intent?

levels.description: Dynamic score refreshed on every signal event, visible in CRM.

name: Lead-to-Account Matching
probe: Are inbound leads matched to the correct account automatically?

levels.description: Automated lead-to-account matching with account-owner routing.
Full matching + de-duping + cross-object relationship mapping.

name: Signal-to-Action Routing
probe: When a signal fires, does it trigger an action automatically?

levels.description: Signals detected but no action taken.
Signals auto-enrol contact into the right sequence.
Multi-channel signal orchestration (sequence + ads + CSM outreach).

name: Inbound Lead Scoring & Routing
probe: How are inbound leads scored and routed?

levels.description: All leads treated equal, one inbox.
Fit + intent score determines SLA tier (hot/warm/nurture).
Full speed-to-lead automation, enriched briefing to rep before first touch.

name: Pipeline Stages & Conversion
probe: Are pipeline stages defined and conversion tracked?

levels.description: Stages documented, conversion rates reviewed monthly.
Exit criteria per stage, conversion tracked by segment/rep.
Stage conversion benchmarks with deviation alerts, deal-desk governance.

name: Lead Routing & Speed-to-Lead
probe: How fast and accurate is inbound lead routing?

levels.description: Sub-5-min SLA with auto-enrichment and rep briefing.

name: Meeting & Scheduling Ops
probe: Is the meeting booking flow friction-free and context-rich?

levels.description: Chili Piper / Calendly routing by owner/tier with prep docs.
Full calendar ops: pre-meeting research auto-generated + reminders + post-meeting sync.

name: Handoff SLAs — Marketing → Sales → CS
probe: Are handoffs governed by SLAs?

levels.description: No SLAs; leads and deals fall through cracks.
Tracked SLAs with alerts on breach.
Automated handoff workflows with full context transfer.

name: Dashboards & Reporting
probe: What operational dashboards exist?

levels.description: Executive BI layer (cross-tool) with predictive / forecasting views.

name: Workflow Automation Library
probe: How mature is the automation layer?

levels.description: Robust n8n / Make library with version control.
AI agents embedded in workflows, full observability, error handling.

name: Renewal & Expansion Pipeline
probe: Is the post-sale motion managed as a proper pipeline?

levels.description: Renewal pipeline in CRM with stage logic.
Forecasted renewals + expansion plays triggered by usage/signals.
Full CS ops motion — health scoring, QBRs, expansion automation.

name: Reply Classification & Disposition
probe: Are outbound replies classified and routed?

levels.description: Replies auto-routed to right flow (meeting / objection handling / nurture).

name: Email Deliverability Infrastructure
probe: Is the sending infrastructure healthy and scalable?

levels.description: 1 domain, a few mailboxes, ad-hoc warmup.
2–5 domains, mailboxes warmed ≥30 days, SPF/DKIM/DMARC set.
Dedicated infra (5+ domains, 3 mailboxes/domain, continuous warmup).
Inbox placement monitoring (GlockApps), reputation alerts, multi-provider redundancy.

name: Sequencer Setup & Cadence
probe: How sophisticated is the outbound sequencer?

levels.description: ICP + signal-specific sequences with A/B testing.
Fully orchestrated sequences across channels with liquid variables and trigger branches.

name: Personalisation Depth
probe: How personalised is outbound messaging at scale?

levels.description: AI-generated personalisation (Clay + Claude) on every send.
Multi-variable relevance (signal + persona + case-study match) — near-1:1 quality at scale.

name: LinkedIn Outbound Operations
probe: How is LinkedIn outbound structured?

levels.description: HeyReach / Expandi / Lemlist with single cadence.
Multi-account LinkedIn outbound, ICP-filtered, tied to CRM.

name: Multi-Channel Orchestration
probe: Are outbound channels coordinated across email, LinkedIn, and ads?

levels.description: Email + LinkedIn run in parallel by same rep.
Email + LinkedIn + ads hitting same ICP accounts in coordinated windows.
Full orchestration — signals trigger cross-channel plays, all in one system.

name: Testing & Iteration Cadence
probe: How systematically is outbound tested and improved?

levels.description: Subject-line / opener A/B tests run ad-hoc.
Weekly performance review with documented test backlog.
Continuous test program — multi-variate on copy, offer, cadence, persona — feeding a learnings doc.

name: Content Engine (Advanced)
probe: Is there a content engine feeding outbound and nurture?

levels.description: Reps send links to old blog posts.
Founder-led content repurposed across email, LinkedIn, ads.
Full Content-as-a-Service: sales conversations → content → ads/outbound/nurture in a closed loop.

name: AI Agents / GTM Ops (Advanced)
probe: Are AI agents embedded in the GTM stack?

levels.description: Custom agents in workflows (enrichment, routing, classification).
AI agents running autonomous plays — research, outbound drafts, reply handling, in background.

name: Paid Strategy & Role in Pipeline
probe: Is there a defined role for paid in the GTM mix?

levels.description: No paid. Or 'we tried it, didn't work.'
Paid runs for brand or lead-gen, measured separately.
Paid has a defined role (demand gen, demand capture, ABM) with targets per channel.
Paid is integrated into the GTM system — budget tied to pipeline targets, feeding outbound and sales.

name: Channel Mix & Budget Allocation
probe: Which paid channels are active, and how is budget allocated?

levels.description: Multi-channel (LinkedIn, Google, Meta) with basic budget logic.
Budget allocated by CAC/ROAS per channel, reviewed monthly.
Portfolio approach — brand, demand capture, ABM, retargeting each with a role and measured ROAS.

name: ABM / Account-Based Ads
probe: Are ads targeting specific high-value accounts?

levels.description: No account targeting — broad audiences only.
ABM audience from target list (top 200 accounts).
ABM ads tied to CRM list, refreshed on new accounts, segmented by tier.
Signal-triggered ABM — ad spend ramps when an account shows intent.

name: Signal-Triggered & Retargeting Flows
probe: Do ads respond to behaviour and intent signals?

levels.description: Retargeting tied to CRM stage + signal source (funding, hiring, intent).
Full signal loop — ads fire on specific triggers, content changes by trigger type, synced with outbound and CSM.

name: Creative & Messaging Quality
probe: How strong is the creative system?

levels.description: Creative system with templates, variants per persona, regular refresh.
Creative atomised from sales conversations and content — persona-matched, tested, refreshed weekly.

name: Landing Page Experience
probe: Where does ad traffic land, and how well does it convert?

levels.description: Segmented landing pages by persona or industry with clear CTAs.
Personalised / account-aware landing pages (dynamic variables, named social proof).

name: Attribution & Measurement
probe: Is paid attribution trustworthy and actionable?

levels.description: Multi-touch attribution model, reviewed monthly, feeds channel decisions.
Blended CAC + pipeline attribution across channels, integrated with CRM forecast.

quantMetric: CAC by channel; CAC payback months

name: Integration with Outbound & CRM
probe: Do ads and outbound work together, or in parallel silos?

levels.description: Ads warm accounts before outbound touches them.
Same account list hit by ads + outbound + content in coordinated windows.
Full orchestration — signals route simultaneously to ads, outbound, and CSM. One pipeline, three channels.

name: Sales Team Structure
probe: How is the sales team structured relative to motion and scale?

levels.description: SDRs (inbound + outbound) + AEs (commercial + enterprise) + ops support.
Full motion: SDRs, AEs, SEs, CS, RevOps, enablement.

name: Qualification Framework
probe: Is there a documented and enforced qualification methodology?

levels.description: MEDDIC / MEDDPICC enforced, fields in CRM.
Qualification scoring drives forecast confidence and deal-desk review.

name: Call Recording & Coaching
probe: Are sales calls recorded, analysed, and used for coaching?

levels.description: AI insights from call analytics feed back into pipeline + sequences + content.

name: Proposal / Quote-to-Cash
probe: How structured is the proposal-to-signature flow?

levels.description: CPQ + approval workflows + e-sign integrated with CRM.
Full quote-to-cash automation with discount governance and auto-provisioning.

name: Pipeline Hygiene & Forecast Accuracy
probe: Is pipeline data trustworthy and is forecast accurate?

levels.description: Pipeline is a mess, no forecasting.
Weekly review, high variance vs actuals.
Weighted forecast by stage with 80%+ accuracy, deal inspection cadence.
Predictive AI forecasting with sub-10% variance, deal-desk governance.

name: Win/Loss Analysis & Closed-Loss Reactivation
probe: Are losses understood and re-engaged when signals change?

levels.description: Quarterly win/loss program with interviews, feedback to product/marketing.
Closed-lost re-engagement flows triggered by signals (tech / personnel / funding change).

name: CRM Deal Management Discipline
probe: How disciplined is CRM data entry by reps?

levels.description: Required fields enforced, activities partially logged.
Every activity auto-logged (email/call/meeting), MEDDIC fields maintained.
CRM is single source; AI summarises calls/emails into CRM automatically.

name: Champion Tracking & Buying Committee Mapping
probe: Are champions and buying committees mapped and tracked?

levels.description: Buying committee mapped in CRM (champion / DM / economic buyer / blockers).
Multi-threaded with champion-moved tracking (Fireflies / Clay alerts on job changes).
